mission.title: keycloak: User attributes with a text more than 255 characters
mission.description: User attributes with a text more than 255 characters. Requested by 30+ users.

steps.title: Check current Keycloak setup
steps.description:
Verify your Keycloak version and database configuration:
```bash
# If running in a container:
docker exec keycloak /opt/keycloak/bin/kc.sh show-config

# Check the container image version:
docker inspect keycloak | grep -i image
```
This issue was resolved in Keycloak 22+ with the new user storage model.

steps.title: Understand the 255-character limitation
steps.description:
In older Keycloak versions, the `USER_ATTRIBUTE` table used `VARCHAR(255)` for the `value` column. This meant any user attribute longer than 255 characters would be silently truncated or cause errors.

This was a significant limitation for use cases such as storing certificates, long JSON payloads, or detailed user metadata in custom attributes.

steps.title: Apply the fix
steps.description:
**For Keycloak 22+ (Quarkus-based, new user storage):**
The new user storage model uses `TEXT` or `CLOB` column types, removing the 255-character limit. Upgrade to Keycloak 22+ and the limitation is resolved automatically.

**For older Keycloak versions (legacy Wildfly-based):**
You can manually alter the database column:
```sql
-- For PostgreSQL:
ALTER TABLE user_attribute ALTER COLUMN value TYPE TEXT;

-- For MySQL/MariaDB:
ALTER TABLE user_attribute MODIFY value TEXT;

-- For H2 (dev only):
ALTER TABLE user_attribute ALTER COLUMN value SET DATA TYPE VARCHAR(4000);
```
See the [source PR](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/16829) for the upstream fix.

steps.title: Verify long attributes are stored correctly
steps.description:
Test that user attributes longer than 255 characters are stored and retrieved correctly:
```bash
# Set a long user attribute via the Admin REST API:
curl -X PUT -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"attributes": {"longattr": ["'$(python3 -c "print('A'*500)")'"]}}' \
  https://your-keycloak/admin/realms/your-realm/users/$USER_ID

# Verify it was stored fully:
curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  https://your-keycloak/admin/realms/your-realm/users/$USER_ID \
  | jq '.attributes.longattr[0] | length'
```
The returned length should be 500, confirming the full attribute was stored.

resolution.summary: The 255-character limit on user attributes was caused by the VARCHAR(255) column type in the USER_ATTRIBUTE table. This was resolved in Keycloak 22+ with the new user storage model which uses TEXT/CLOB column types. For older versions, the database column can be manually altered to support longer values.
codeSnippets: ALTER TABLE user_attribute ALTER COLUMN value TYPE TEXT;

prerequisites.description: A running Keycloak server (via Docker/Podman or Kubernetes with the Keycloak Operator) with database access for schema changes on older versions.
